issues.title: EXP-450 P2: EM-incorrect 1/r decay claim in 2.14 ring-magnet subsection
issues.description: The proposed 2.14 ring-magnet subsection third paragraph asserts "1/r-type decay (or 1/r² in spherically symmetric cases)" — this is wrong. Magnetic dipole far-field decays as 1/r³. Dome defenders can use this to discredit the entire proposal. Fix: delete the third paragraph entirely; paragraphs 1-2 stand on their own.
issues.suggested_fix: Delete P2 third paragraph ("The exponential form is also incompatible..."). The flux-conservation argument in paragraphs 1-2 is the strongest version of WIN-053. If a decay argument is desired, use 1/r³ dipole far-field.

Of these, Part 1 alone has 3 anchor refs affecting build-scripts/generate-html.js. P4 operations need a Step 5 sweep list or the decider must track all renaming sites.

issues.title: EXP-450 P1: 2.13 κ divide-by-zero metaphor is literal, not pedagogical
issues.description: P1 2.13 subsection headline uses "treating it as 1.67 nT/µGal is treating 1/0 as a finite number" — this is a compression metaphor that dome defenders can attack as technically inaccurate. The actual argument is that the coupling constant's denominator is contradicted by the dome's own later high-precision data.
issues.suggested_fix: Soften to: "...A coupling constant whose denominator is contradicted by the dome's own later high-precision confirmations cannot be treated as a fixed structural coefficient at the precision the dome itself cites." Remove "treating 1/0 as a finite number" framing.

issues.title: EXP-451 P2: 22.6 Hz worked example misattributes source — should be 8.77 Hz pole-floor or name averaging explicitly
issues.description: P2 worked-example says dome predicts 22.6 Hz from "flat-plate resonator with published exponentially-decaying firmament profile." But existing review (line 1063) attributes 22.6 Hz to the linear radial average H_eff = H₀·(L/R)·(1−exp(−R/L)) ≈ 3,316 km — an averaging convention. H(0) = 8,537 km gives 8.77 Hz (the unambiguous pole-floor). Using 22.6 Hz without naming the averaging convention mirrors the dome's own formula-switching behavior.
issues.suggested_fix: Option A (cleaner): use H(0) = 8,537 km → f = c/(4H) = 8.77 Hz, no averaging needed. Option B: keep 22.6 Hz but explicitly name the integration scheme: "the linear-radial-averaged effective height H_eff ≈ 3,316 km in the quarter-wave formula".

issues.title: WIN-023: Add t=0 anchor looseness clause to defend against "5% miss" objection
issues.description: WIN-023 verdict dismisses theological link as unfalsifiable but does not say WHY the 1000-year target band is loose. A close reader could counter "Revelation 20:7 says 1000 years and SAA formed at 950 AD — that is a 5% miss, why not impressive?" The answer (t=0 has multiple candidate starts — resurrection AD33, Constantine AD312, Theodosius AD380 → band AD1033-1380, ~350yr wide) is not stated. Adding one sentence hardens the unfalsifiability verdict.
issues.routing_reason: Argument hardening for WIN-023 verdict requires scientific judgment about why 1000-year band is loose. Cannot narrow-patch without risking oversimplification.

The carry-over means c2's recommendation was not applied.
issues.routing_reason: Carry-over from c2/c3: substantive argument about firmament cavity geometry vs room dimensions for acoustic eigenmodes. Requires scientific analysis beyond narrow patch.

The defense survives without this but is weaker than necessary.
issues.routing_reason: WIN-066 carry-forward: Counter (1) needs specific mechanical link for globe physics producing observed pattern. Requires scientific specificity.

issues.title: Template the live-fetch paragraph in §2b.1 to use listOfWinIds helper (exposed by EXP-442/ISS-2322 recommendation)
issues.description: The paragraph beginning Even the {{CA_LIVE}} live-fetch WINs are not independent (sections.json part2b ~offset 1223) hardcodes four WIN IDs (004, 006, 007, 024) and says All four data sources, but WIN-004 and WIN-024 are NOT monitoring=live_fetch in current wins.json — only WIN-006 and WIN-007 are. The contradiction is glaring next to the correctly-templated contrast paragraph. Also update the opening count from the 2 live-fetch WINs to {{CA_LIVE}} live-fetch WINs if not already templated.

issues.title: WIN-004 c8 H1: systemic TLDR HTML markup bug in WIN-052/053/064/070
issues.description: WIN-004 c7 H1 (TLDR HTML markup renders as escaped text) was fixed for WIN-004 only via per-WIN content patch. Systemic rendering bug at build-scripts/generate-html.js lines ~828 and ~840 remains. WIN-052, WIN-053, WIN-064, WIN-070 also have HTML in tldr fields that will render as escaped text. Two paths: (a) cohort patch — rewrite WIN-052/053/064/070 tldr fields to plain text; (b) pipeline fix — fix the rendering at generate-html.js so HTML is injected, not escaped.

issues.suggested_fix: Path (a): change WIN-052/053/064/070 tldr_evidence/tldr_verdict fields to plain-text equivalents (no HTML tags). Path (b): update generate-html.js to use innerHTML or equivalent when rendering TLDR fields that may contain markup.

issues.title: WIN-004 c8 H2: awkward phrasing "drift of the South Atlantic anomaly westward regime"
issues.description: WIN-004 detail_evidence introduced awkward phrasing: "is consistent with the drift of the South Atlantic anomaly westward regime modeled by core-flow MHD". Curmudgeon recommends: "is consistent with the westward-drift regime of the South Atlantic anomaly modeled by core-flow MHD".
issues.suggested_fix: Replace "is consistent with the drift of the South Atlantic anomaly westward regime modeled by core-flow MHD" with "is consistent with the westward-drift regime of the South Atlantic anomaly modeled by core-flow MHD".

issues.title: SEC-7.11 c1 H1: critical-angle calculation error — 16.7° should be ~20.6°
issues.description: §7.11 paragraph 1 states "total internal reflection at angles below arcsin(1/n) ≈ 16.7° at the ice wall." Correct formula: critical angle = arcsin(1/n) where n≈2.84 (ice wall), giving arcsin(1/2.84) ≈ 20.6°, not 16.7°. The 16.7° figure corresponds to n≈3.47, not the stated n≈2.84.
issues.suggested_fix: Replace "arcsin(1/n) ≈ 16.7°" with "arcsin(1/n) ≈ 20.6°" (or rephrase: "the critical angle from inside the n≈2.84 medium is ~20.6°"). Verify arithmetic: arcsin(1/2.84) = arcsin(0.352) ≈ 20.6°.

issues.title: SEC-7.11 c1 H2: equatorial ring "requires k ≈ 0.097" inconsistent with n=1.40 formula
issues.description: §7.11 claims "equatorial ring table value n = 1.40 requires ~0.097" but this is inconsistent with the dome's n(r) formula at r ≈ 10,008 km (equator). With n(r) and n=1.40, the required k ≈ 0.18, not 0.097. Needs verification of the r value the dome Height Table associates with n=1.40.
issues.location: data/sections.json: SEC-7.11 refraction table analysis paragraph
issues.suggested_fix: Verify the r value the dome Height Table associates with n=1.40. If r=10,008 km: state "requires k ≈ 0.18" and drop the 0.097 number. If r is elsewhere, state the correct r and recalculate.

issues.title: SEC-7.11 c1 H3: chromatic dispersion falsification framing needs n(r) wavelength caveat
issues.description: §7.11 treats chromatic dispersion (rainbow smearing) as an automatic prediction of n≈2.84, but the dome's n(r) is written without explicit wavelength-dependence — a constant-n medium would have zero dispersion. The falsification framing is logically incomplete.
issues.suggested_fix: Qualify with either: "a refractive medium with wavelength-dependent n produces dispersion; if aether is wavelength-independent by construction, chromatic dispersion would not be predicted." Or: state the dome has not committed to wavelength-dependence so dispersion is only a conditional prediction.

issues.title: SEC-7.11 c1 H4: WIN-017/WIN-026 equivalence claim needs per-WIN supporting half-sentence
issues.description: §7.11 WIN-level impact paragraph lists WIN-017 (parallax via firmament wobble) and WIN-026 (crepuscular rays via perspective) as requiring equivalent unfalsifiable corrections to n(r), but does not explain the shared structural property that makes them equivalent.
issues.suggested_fix: Either (a) add half-sentence per WIN explaining shared structural property ("each invokes a free dome-specific parameter unavailable in the globe model"), or (b) weaken the claim: "may require similar free-parameter corrections".

issues.description: EXP-516 patches P6 and P7 target build-scripts/generate-html.js (infrastructure file, cannot be self-applied by decider). P6 changes the dome_status_current span to add italic+tooltip clarifying "Our tracking status, inferred from commit diffs..." rather than implying dome labels. P7 changes the count paragraph from "visibly disowned — marked [REMOVED] or [SUSPENDED]" to "effectively disowned — silently removed" framing. Both patches have verified find/replace text in EXP-516-ISS-2373-bracket-strawman.json. Human: apply P6+P7 from that file to build-scripts/generate-html.js, run node build.js html + node test.js.

issues.title: renderFailureEntry "Dome's label:" prose-prefix misattributes internal tracking shorthand to dome
issues.description:
In build-scripts/generate-html.js renderFailureEntry, each silent-failure card displays "Dome's label: REMOVED 2026-03-15" (or SUSPENDED). The dome_label field is OUR internal tracking shorthand (we observed the removal via commit diff; the date is our observation). The prose-prefix "Dome's label:" attributes this string to the dome as if the dome publishes these labels. Fix: rename to "Effective status (our tracking label):" in generate-html.js. Verified find:

Dome's label: | replace:

Effective status (our tracking label):. Infrastructure file — human implementation needed.
issues.escalation_reason: generate-html.js is infrastructure file — decider self-apply restriction applies.

issues.title: EXP-481: uncounted-failures.json dome_accuracy_formula has 2 vestigial bare 69s post-integration
issues.description: After EXP-481 lands dome_claimed_confirmed:69 in uncounted-failures.json, the same file retains dome_accuracy_formula:"69 / (69 + 4)" with two bare 69s. If the dome bumps to 70, dome_claimed_confirmed updates but dome_accuracy_formula stays "69 / (69 + 4)" — inconsistent within the same file. Mitigating factor: dome_accuracy_formula has zero grep hits in build-scripts/ or data/ so it is unrendered. Still: the very anti-pattern EXP-481 exists to prevent, reproduced inside EXP-481's own change. Fix: update or remove dome_accuracy_formula field to not contain bare literals.

issues.title: EXP-481: test_recommendations item 1 regression test lacks expected whitelist count
issues.description: test_recommendations item 1 says "Grep \b69\b in rendered docs/index.html post-build; assert match count matches the explicit whitelist" but does not supply the expected count number (~16-18 post-patches). The pseudocode leaves expected_whitelist_count as a TODO. The decider/engineer implementing this test must compute and enshrine the count independently.

issues.title: EXP-481: anticipated_objections #3 partially false — poller does not write to uncounted-failures.json
issues.description: anticipated_objections #3 claims dome_claimed_confirmed will be auto-maintained because "the refresh agent's job description already includes tracking the dome's published scoreboard." But: (a) the poller WATCHES the count and fires breaking_news on delta but does NOT write to uncounted-failures.json; (b) that file's last_updated is over a month stale. The test that would detect staleness (test_recommendations item 4) points at monitor/status.json as source-of-truth, but status.json does not carry the dome's confirmed-count. The auto-maintenance claim is not implemented.

issues.title: EXP-481: holes_NOT_addressed_here item 4 undercounts generate-html.js bare-69 sites (4 sites, not 2)
issues.description: EXP-481 cites 2 bare-69 sites in generate-html.js Timestamp Error tab (ts-april-2026-update). Independent scan finds 4 distinct sites: (i) line 1981 TLDR, (ii) line 1987 detail-body (two instances), (iii) line 1995 closing, (iv) line 2001 related-callout. The next EXP picking up this scope should target 4 sites, not 2.

issues.title: EXP-481: change_6 ({{HONEST_ACCURACY}} description) marked optional despite containing 2 bare 69s that will become stale
issues.description: change_6 updates _meta.placeholders.{{HONEST_ACCURACY}} description to use {{DOME_CONFIRMED_HEADLINE}} instead of bare "69 / (69 + dome_claimed_failures + silent_failures)". Marked "Optional follow-up." The description string contains TWO bare 69s that become stale on the same trigger as the prose 69s — directly contradicting the discipline EXP-481 establishes. Promote change_6 from optional to required.

issues.title: EXP-481: part4 three-class breakdown deferral weak — 2/3 plumbing already exists
issues.description: mentions_left_unchanged item 2 defers the part4 "9 of 83 registry items (69 confirmed, 4 refined, 10 prospective)" three-class breakdown. But dome_claimed_prospective_count=10 is already in uncounted-failures.json and domeProspectiveCount is already in generate-html.js context. After EXP-481 adds dome_claimed_confirmed, only dome_claimed_refined is missing. The deferral leaves 1 site bare when 2-line change closes it. Defensible scope discipline but the asymmetry argument is underdeveloped.
